title: Kid’s School Desk
summary: The Iconic Children's School Desk is a perfect place for the kids to do their homework, or even perhaps write their letters to Santa This holiday season.
description: The Iconic Children's School Desk is a perfect place for the kids to do their homework, or even perhaps write their letters to Santa This holiday season.

steps.title: Cut List
steps.body:
Step 1. ​​Make all the necessary cuts as provided in the plans.
TIP: When cutting cabinet grade finished Oak Plywood, use 40T-60T Saw blade as well as masking tape curing crosscuts. (cutting across the grain)

steps.title: Taper Cut
steps.body:
Taper Sides of the Storage component using a Taper jig.
Tip​​. I built my Taper jig from leftover scraps and mounted the Kreg Bench vice to it the secure the pieces when running through the table saw​.

steps.title: Pocket Holes
steps.body: ​​Create Pocket holes that would be secured on the inside and the underneath side of the storage component.

steps.title: Assemble
steps.body:
​​Assemble using 1-1⁄4” Coarse Kreg Pocket screws along with wood glue.
Tip​​. Use a Kreg Right Angle clamp to secure your pieces in place before driving the screws in to prevent movement out of place.

steps.title: Lid Dry Fit
steps.body:
​​Dry Fit the top lid to ensure proper size and grain direction.
Tip. ​​When securing the pieces with wood glue and pocket screws, lay down masking tape on all the Butt-Joints the catch glue squeeze out and save you hours on cleanup

steps.title: Tape
steps.body: To create the chair frame, sandwich together with masking tape the two 11”x 16” pieces and draw your desired chair frame.

steps.title: Chair Frame
steps.body:
Cut the sandwich together chair frame pieces on your bandsaw.
Tip. ​​Clean up the blade marks left from your bandsaw using a belt or spindle sander.

steps.title: Clean up Saw Marks
steps.body: Clean up the blade marks left from your bandsaw using a belt or spindle sander.

steps.title: Back and Bottom Rest
steps.body: Cut the Backrest (9-1⁄2 x 11-1⁄2”) and the bottom seat (9-1⁄2” x 11-1⁄2”) and secure using pocket holes and wood glue.

steps.title: Back and Bottom Rest
steps.body: Cut the Backrest (9-1⁄2 x 11-1⁄2”) and the bottom seat (9-1⁄2” x 11-1⁄2”) and secure using pocket holes and wood glue.

steps.title: Base Cuts
steps.body: Cut the pieces for the table base from the plans above. ● (x2) 16” ● (x1) 21” ● (x2) 16” ● (x2) 8” ● (x2) 11-1⁄2” ● (x1) 8”

steps.title: Base Posts
steps.body: ​​To create the posts, glue and clamp the (x2) 16” pieces and the (x2) 8” pieces. Set to dry for 1-2 hours in optimum drying conditions.

steps.title: Trim Legs
steps.body: ​​Trim all of the table base pieces, including the glued up posts (once dried) down to 3” final width, cutting off the rounded over edges on your table saw.

steps.title: Trim Legs
steps.body: ​​Trim all of the table base pieces, including the glued up posts (once dried) down to 3” final width, cutting off the rounded over edges on your table saw.

steps.title: Tee Suport
steps.body: Create the “T-Support” using the 8” and the 11-1⁄2” piece. Secure with Wood Glue and 2-1⁄2” Pocket screws.

steps.title: Base Assembly
steps.body:
Assemble the base with Wood Glue and 2-1/2” Pocket screws.
Tip​​.Secure the screws from the bottom of the legs, that way they will be hidden.

steps.title: Post attachment
steps.body:
​​Secure the posts using wood glue and (x4) 3” screws.
When securing the “Tee-Support”, align the seam of the “Butt-Joint” to sit in the middle of the 3”x3” post for strength.

steps.title: Tee Support install
steps.body: Attach the Tee support in the middle of the 3x3 post (at the joint of the Tee) with x3 three inch screws

steps.title: Tee Support install
steps.body: Attach the Tee support in the middle of the 3x3 post (at the joint of the Tee) with x3 three inch screws

steps.title: Seat Attachment
steps.body: Attach the seat using (x6-x8) 1-1⁄4” screws from the bottom. To prevent flex, stager the screws.

steps.title: Storage desk Install
steps.body: When attaching the Storage component to the “Tee-Support” drive (x6) 1-3⁄4” screws.

steps.title: Anchored Lid
steps.body: Attach the 4” wide piece to the top of the storage component via wood glue and clamps.

steps.title: Edge Banding
steps.body: Using Oak or Birch Iron-on Edge banding, cut each piece to size and conceal all the exposed plywood edges.

steps.title: Trim Edge Banding
steps.body: Using a scrap piece of wood wrapped with 150 grit sandpaper, sand flush all the edges of the edge banded veneer.

steps.title: Putty and Sand
steps.body: After patching up any of the knots found in the Douglas Fir with paintable wood putty, sand the entire project down to 150 grit.

steps.title: Hinge
steps.body: Attach the 12” Piano hinge using the provided screws.

steps.title: Stain
steps.body: Stain the Writing/Storage compartment along with the chairs.

steps.title: Paint and Primer
steps.body: Paint the base using a paint and primer.

steps.title: Clear Coat
steps.body: Spray or wipe on the water based satin polyurethane for protection​.
